The efficacy of clopidogrel in preventing myocardial infarction and stroke was evaluated in a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial. In this study, 12,664 patients were randomly assigned to receive either clopidogrel or a placebo. The primary endpoint was the occurrence of myocardial infarction or stroke. The results showed that the incidence of the primary endpoint was significantly lower in the clopidogrel group than in the placebo group. The study also found that the incidence of major cardiovascular events was significantly lower in the clopidogrel group than in the placebo group. In addition, the study found that the incidence of major bleeding was significantly lower in the clopidogrel group than in the placebo group. The study concluded that clopidogrel is effective in preventing myocardial infarction and stroke in patients undergoing PCI.
